_comment: Trust anchors are pinned by the operator running the verifier — the implementer being checked never supplies them (ADR-010). Keys are matched by issuer NAMESPACE (grant.iss). Today the issuer namespace is founder-operated; the future change is only WHERE it resolves and WHO governs this file, not the verifier.

https://interim-fiduciary.kilbowieconsul.public_key_pem:
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
<issuer status-list + issuance key>
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----
https://interim-fiduciary.kilbowieconsul.custodian: founder-operated (interim) — moves to a rights-holder fiduciary; see ADR-010

urn:h2a:subject:jane-actor-001.public_key_pem:
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
<subject consent key, as enrolled>
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

bridle.public_key_pem:
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
<Bridle evidence-signing key — the party being checked>
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

_comment_l3: L3 anchoring (ADR-005). Pinned by the authority/witness names carried in an anchor (GET /v0/anchor-keys). `qualified:true` asserts the TSA certificate is eIDAS-qualified — the interim reference TSA is NOT qualified, so it is pinned qualified:false and reaches L3-interim only.

urn:h2a:tsa:reference.public_key_pem:
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
<TSA key — a real eIDAS-qualified TSA cert in production>
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

urn:h2a:witness:reference.public_key_pem:
-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----
<independent witness key>
-----END PUBLIC KEY-----
urn:h2a:witness:reference.custodian: founder-operated (interim) — moves to a fiduciary-operated witness; see ADR-005
